value.tone: Declarative. Terse. Developer-first. No marketing fluff.
value.person: Second person (you/your) when addressing the reader; avoid first-person plural marketing voice.
style_rules: No em dashes. Use periods, commas, semicolons, colons, parentheses, or middle-dot.
No 'not just X, Y' constructions. Say what the thing is.
No hype verbs: seamlessly, unlock, supercharge, empower, revolutionize, leverage, delve, harness, elevate, streamline.
No hedging: 'tends to be', 'typically', 'most teams find'. State the fact or say it's uncertain plainly.
Active voice. Short sentences. Concrete nouns over abstract.
Numbers beat adjectives. 'Under 1 second' beats 'blazing fast'.
Code blocks with real, working examples. No pseudo-code unless explicitly framed as such.

structure.headers: H2 for major sections, H3 for sub-sections. Front-load the takeaway in the intro.
structure.code: Real CLI commands, real HCL, real SQL. Always specify the database/version.
structure.diagrams: Mermaid for flow diagrams. Plain ASCII for state tables.
structure.meta_description: Under 155 chars, single sentence, no clickbait.

do_not_publish: Vendor comparisons that name competitors directly (use 'traditional ETL tools', 'batch-oriented platforms')

value.differentiator: Sub-second data movement (<1s) with full schema/index/pipeline IaC via Kolumn (free).

capture_modes.name: MySQL Binlog
capture_modes.description: Row-based binlog tailing, ordered stream output.

capture_modes.name: MSSQL CDC
capture_modes.description: Change tracking via change tables, polling-based.

capture_modes.name: Outbox Pattern
capture_modes.description: App writes events to outbox table; bridge tails and forwards.

agent_data_layer.description: Per-workspace PostgreSQL + pgvector + AGE for agent records, memory, graph, semantic search.
agent_data_layer.agents: Hosted via OpenCLAW runtime; Anthropic and OpenAI via virtual key proxy (ClawShell).

kolumn.description: Free IaC tool for database schemas, indexes, transformations, and SchemaBounce pipelines.
kolumn.syntax: HCL with create/discover blocks. Not Terraform 'resource' syntax.
kolumn.vs_dbt: Manages schemas + indexes + pipelines, not just SQL transforms; runs <1s on CDC events, not after batch loads.

value.ADL: Agent Data Layer. Per-workspace PostgreSQL with pgvector and AGE extensions for agent state, memory, semantic search, and graph relationships.
value.Asynq: Redis-backed Go task queue used for all async work. Tasks live in asynq:{queue}:pending, never raw Redis lists.
value.CDC: Change Data Capture. Streaming row-level changes from a source database in near-real-time.
value.ClawShell: Rust sidecar that proxies LLM calls. Resolves virtual keys (vk_anthropic_ws_*) to real API keys.
value.Crystallization: OpenCLAW pattern detection that promotes repeated tool sequences into zero-token skills.
value.Direct Connect: Pull-based ingestion mode. SchemaBounce queries the source on a schedule.
value.Egress mode: Bot-level setting controlling outbound network calls: 'none', 'allowlist', or 'unrestricted'.
value.Environment: A logical pipeline isolation boundary inside a workspace. Each environment has its own Redis and worker.
value.ingest API: Public HTTPS endpoint that receives external events (webhooks, agents) and forwards to the per-environment Redis.
value.Kolumn: SchemaBounce's IaC tool. Free. HCL syntax. Manages databases, schemas, indexes, transforms, pipelines.
value.MCP: Model Context Protocol. SchemaBounce exposes a public MCP for read-only data tools.
value.North Star: A bot's read-only seed memory: brand voice, product catalog, glossary, off-limits. Loaded into ADL Zone1.
value.OpenCLAW: The agent runtime. Hosts bots, executes their run protocols, manages crystallization.
value.Outbox pattern: Transactional event capture via an app-managed outbox table; bridge tails it.
value.pgvector: PostgreSQL extension for vector similarity search; backs ADL semantic_search.
value.Pipeline worker: Unified per-environment binary that handles both CDC ingestion and sink delivery.
value.Route: A SchemaBounce pipeline route maps one source's events to one or more sinks with optional transforms.
value.SaaS connector: JSON-defined source connector for SaaS APIs (Stripe, HubSpot, etc.). 100+ connectors built in.
value.schemabounce-api: The control-plane HTTP API. Lives at /api/v1/*. Handles workspaces, routes, sinks, billing, ADL.
value.Sink: A destination for CDC events. 50+ types: Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Kafka, S3, etc.
value.Skill (ADL): A reusable prompt + tool-requirements bundle baked into a bot at deploy time.
value.Source: A producer of CDC events: a database, an outbox table, a webhook, or a SaaS API.
value.Workspace: Top-level tenancy boundary. Each workspace has its own ADL, environments, billing.

value.framing: Use generic categories, not vendor names, in published copy.

categories.name: batch ETL platforms
categories.framing: Batch-oriented loaders with 5 to 15 minute latency.

categories.name: transform-only tools
categories.framing: SQL transformations that run after data already arrived.

categories.name: streaming ETL
categories.framing: Faster than batch but bounded by their connector roadmap.

value.our_position: Sub-second CDC with full IaC. Kolumn is free. Most pipelines that take 15 minutes elsewhere take under a second here.

do_not_say: SOC 2 compliant (we are not yet; use 'SOC 2 in progress' if mentioned at all)
PCI DSS compliant (we are pursuing certification; do not claim it)
the fastest CDC platform (no public benchmark)
SchemaBounce is the only X (almost never true)
do_not_publish_about: internal agent prompts or system prompts (treat as proprietary)
customer names without explicit written permission
infrastructure provider names beyond what is on the public stack page
tone_traps_we_have_committed_before: AI-speak: 'Imagine a world where...', 'In today's fast-paced digital landscape...'
Tricolons that say nothing: 'Faster, smarter, simpler.'
Vague benefit verbs without a measurement attached
